policy_statement: By 2025, review and evaluate the EDTTCC based on the Long-Term Climate Strategy and current climate plans (IM2a-2025).
mechanism_description: Chile will review and evaluate its EDTTCC using the Long-Term Climate Strategy and current climate plans to support improved climate governance and planning capacity.
primary_causal_pathway: Governance process improvement (review/M&E) → ↑ institutional adaptive capacity → ↓ climate risk via better planning/implementation
causal_mechanism_detail: (1) Changes the governance system by instituting periodic review and evaluation of the climate strategy against long-term strategy and existing plans. (2) The mediating process is organizational learning and adaptive management: identifying gaps, updating targets/measures, improving coordination, budgeting, and accountability through monitoring and evaluation. (3) The climate-relevant outcome is improved preparedness and effectiveness of adaptation planning and implementation, reducing vulnerability and expected damages from climate hazards over time.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is strengthened institutional adaptive capacity through monitoring, evaluation, and iterative improvement of climate governance and plans.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: review/evaluation → better governance capacity (adaptive management) → improved ability to manage climate risks.  
2) Why Adaptation over alternatives: while reviews can also refine mitigation actions, the mechanism specified is “institutional adaptive capacity,” whose primary climate effect is enabling more effective adaptation decisions and implementation under uncertainty.  
3) Edge cases: if a review is explicitly aimed at tightening emissions targets or correcting mitigation underperformance, it could be mitigation-dominant at the policy level; however, that is not inherent to this canonical mechanism as stated.  
4) Applies to ALL instances: any policy that institutionalizes climate strategy review/evaluation to improve governance/planning capacity shares this adaptation-capacity pathway as its main climate-relevant effect.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment (e.g., whether it supports mitigation planning, finance readiness, or specific sectoral resilience) is deferred to policy-level evidence.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: Could be dual-benefit (mitigation + adaptation) depending on what the evaluation prioritizes; classification stays Adaptation because the canonical mechanism targets institutional adaptive capacity rather than direct emissions or efficiency outcomes.
key_indicators: “By 2025”; “reviewed and evaluated”; “based on the Long-Term Climate Strategy”; “current climate plans”; “EDTTCC”
instance_edge_case_notes: Could also be interpreted as a target-setting instrument due to the deadline (“By 2025”), but the operative action is an evaluation/review process (planning/M&E), not an outcome target.
